project.name: Sublemonable
project.description: Zero-knowledge, end-to-end encrypted ephemeral messaging app for browser, iOS, and Android

security.philosophy: Zero-knowledge architecture. The server never sees, stores, or logs plaintext message content under any circumstances.

protected_against: Server compromise — messages encrypted before leaving device
Forward secrecy breach — Double Ratchet key rotation per message
Screenshot leaks — platform-specific prevention and detection
Metadata surveillance — minimal metadata, optional Tor routing
Replay attacks — message nonces and timestamp validation
Brute force — Argon2id key derivation for all passwords
out_of_scope: Full OS-level screenshot prevention on browser

key_types.identity_key: Curve25519 long-term keypair, generated on device, never leaves device
key_types.signed_prekey: Curve25519, rotated every 7 days, signed by identity key
key_types.one_time_prekeys: Curve25519, batch of 100 uploaded to server (public keys only), consumed once
key_types.session_keys: Derived via X3DH on first message, Double Ratchet thereafter
key_types.message_keys: AES-256-GCM, derived per message, discarded after use

key_storage.web: IndexedDB encrypted with AES-256-GCM, master key derived from passphrase via Argon2id
key_storage.android: Android Keystore System, hardware-backed where available

certificate_pinning.ios: NSURLSession with pinned certificate SHA-256 hash
certificate_pinning.web: Service Worker intercept with HPKP-style validation

tor.method: Orbot integration on mobile, .onion address for browser via Tor Browser

what_is_stored: Encrypted message envelopes (blob only, no metadata)
Delivery receipts (hash of message ID only)

message_persistence.lifetime: Messages deleted from server immediately upon delivery confirmation
message_persistence.no_delivery_action: Message purged after TTL, sender notified

api.style: REST for auth/registration + WebSocket for messaging
api.authentication: JWT (RS256, 15min expiry) + refresh token (7 days, rotated on use)

endpoints.POST /api/v1/register: Create account — accepts public identity key + prekeys only
endpoints.GET /api/v1/users/:id/prekey: Fetch one-time prekey bundle for X3DH
endpoints.POST /api/v1/prekeys: Upload new batch of one-time prekeys
endpoints.GET /api/v1/prekeys/count: Check prekey stock (triggers client to upload more)
endpoints.WS /ws: Authenticated WebSocket for real-time message delivery
endpoints.DELETE /api/v1/account: Full account deletion — purges all data

client_to_server: message.burn — request early destruction of a message

logging.application_logs: Errors and system events only — no request body logging, no user-identifiable data

crypto.description: Shared crypto primitives used by web client. Wraps libsodium.js.

protocol.description: Shared message format, types, and serialization (used by web + server TS types)

message_envelope_schema.ephemeral_key: base64 Curve25519 public key (X3DH only, null after)
message_envelope_schema.ttl_seconds: number | null — null means no self-destruct

ui.description: Shared React Native + React component library
components: MessageBubble — renders with blur overlay when capture detected

web.key_storage: IndexedDB via idb library, AES-256-GCM encrypted

pwa.offline_support: Service Worker — queues outbound messages, caches UI only

screenshot_protection.action: Apply CSS filter: blur(24px) + grayscale(1) to #messages-container

watermark.method: Canvas steganography — encode recipient_id + timestamp into message background
watermark.purpose: Leak attribution — if screenshot shared, watermark identifies recipient

features: No clipboard access to message text (CSS user-select: none)
DevTools open detection with session warning

ios.key_storage: iOS Keychain + Secure Enclave for identity key

screen_recording_detection.action: Immediately overlay UIVisualEffectView (blur) over message list

screenshot_detection.action: Show warning banner + log event locally
screenshot_detection.note: Fires after screenshot taken — cannot prevent, only react

screenshot_protection.secure_text_fields: All message text rendered in custom UIView with isSecureTextEntry-style protection where possible

features: Ephemeral messages with local + server TTL sync
No message preview in iOS notification center
Notification content extension — shows 'New Message' only, no content
Optional Tor via Orbot URL scheme integration

android.key_storage: Android Keystore System (hardware-backed on supported devices)

screenshot_protection.scope: Applied to all Activities containing message content
screenshot_protection.effect: OS-level hard block — screenshots and screen recording show black screen
screenshot_protection.note: Strongest protection of all three platforms

features: No notification content in lock screen
Optional Tor via Orbot intent integration
App masking — ability to disguise app icon

images.encryption: Encrypted as binary blob using same Signal Protocol envelope
images.storage: Never stored on server. Client-to-client direct encrypted transfer via relay

burn_on_read.description: Message destroyed on all devices immediately after first open

typing_indicators.note: Typing events sent as encrypted signals, not plaintext

read_receipts.implementation: Encrypted signal — server never knows read status

group_messaging.note: Phase 2 — requires Sender Keys protocol extension

accounts.username: Optional display name, not used for routing
accounts.routing_id: UUID — never exposed to other users directly
accounts.discovery: QR code exchange or direct link (no phone number, no email required)

contact_verification.method: Safety Number comparison (SHA-512 fingerprint of both identity keys)

platform_warning_system.description: Informs users when their contact is on a platform with reduced screenshot protection. UI layer only — no changes to crypto or server message architecture.
platform_warning_system.purpose: Browser clients cannot block screenshots at the OS level. Contacts on native apps deserve to know this so they can make informed decisions about what to share.

platform_tracking.where: Session metadata — not message content. E2EE is not affected.
platform_tracking.server_stores: Platform string per active session — treated as non-sensitive session metadata
platform_tracking.passed_to_contact: Via encrypted contact/conversation info signal — not embedded in message envelopes
platform_tracking.real_time_updates: If contact switches platforms mid-conversation (e.g. opens web client while mobile is active), warning updates in real time via WebSocket event

warning_display.location: Persistent badge in conversation header, below contact name
warning_display.copy_browser_contact: 🌐 Messaging via browser — screenshot protection unavailable
warning_display.copy_own_browser: 🌐 You're on browser — your messages can be screenshotted
warning_display.dismiss_scope: Per-conversation — dismissing on one conversation does not affect others
warning_display.re_shown: Re-shown if contact switches to browser after being dismissed
warning_display.color: Lemon yellow warning tone — not red, not alarming, informative

who_sees_warning.native_app_user: Sees warning when their contact is on browser
who_sees_warning.browser_user: Sees warning about their own limitations — honest and upfront
who_sees_warning.both_on_browser: Single shared warning shown to both parties

non_intrusive_design.no_modal: Never blocks the UI with a modal or forced acknowledgement
non_intrusive_design.no_alarm: Warning tone is informative not alarming — matches lemon yellow brand
non_intrusive_design.one_time_per_session: After dismissal, only re-appears if platform changes

privacy_view_mode.description: Physical shoulder-surfing defense. Message content blurred by default, revealed only while actively interacting. Captures the blurred state on browser screenshot attempts.
privacy_view_mode.purpose: Protects against someone physically looking at your screen in public — on a train, in a cafe, over your shoulder.

scope.global_toggle: Settings → Privacy → Privacy View — applies to all conversations
scope.per_conversation: Long press conversation → Enable Privacy View — applies to that conversation only
scope.conversation_list: When privacy view active, conversation list previews also blurred — no message content visible on main screen

reveal_modes.description: User selects preferred reveal interaction in Settings → Privacy → Privacy View → Reveal Mode

hold_to_reveal.name: Hold to reveal
hold_to_reveal.description: Content blurred by default. Hold finger/mouse on message area to reveal. Release to re-blur. Most secure.
hold_to_reveal.best_for: High-risk environments, public transport, shared spaces

tap_timed.name: Tap to reveal (timed)
tap_timed.description: Tap to reveal for configurable duration (5s / 10s / 30s), then auto-re-blurs

tap_toggle.name: Tap to toggle
tap_toggle.description: Tap once to reveal, tap again to blur. Stays revealed until tapped again.
tap_toggle.best_for: Low-risk environments where user wants occasional protection

visual_design.blur_style: Frosted lemon-citrus texture overlay — not a generic grey blur
visual_design.texture: Semi-transparent lemon-yellow frosted glass effect — CSS backdrop-filter: blur(20px) with lemon tint rgba(245,230,66,0.08)
visual_design.intentional_look: Looks like a deliberate design choice, not a broken UI — on-brand and intentional
visual_design.bubble_shape_preserved: Message bubble shapes and layout remain visible — only text content is obscured
visual_design.reveal_animation: Smooth 150ms blur-out on reveal, 200ms blur-in on re-blur — fast enough to feel responsive

screenshot_behavior.android: FLAG_SECURE still active — screenshot shows black screen regardless of privacy view state
screenshot_behavior.ios: Screenshot detection triggers blur overlay. If privacy view already active, screenshot captures blurred state — partial mitigation.
screenshot_behavior.browser: Screenshots capture whatever is currently on screen. If privacy view active and content blurred, screenshot captures blurred state. This is the best mitigation available on browser.

notification_behavior.when_privacy_view_active: Push notifications show sender name only — no message preview regardless of OS notification settings
notification_behavior.lock_screen: No message content on lock screen when privacy view enabled for that conversation

platform_implementation.web: CSS backdrop-filter on message container. Hold event via mousedown/touchstart + mouseup/touchend. Privacy view state stored in encrypted local settings.
platform_implementation.ios: SwiftUI blur modifier with custom lemon tint. Long press gesture recognizer for hold-to-reveal. UINotificationFeedbackGenerator on reveal for tactile confirmation.
platform_implementation.android: Jetpack Compose BlurMaskFilter with lemon tint overlay. pointerInput for hold detection. HapticFeedbackType.LongPress on reveal.

indicator.active_indicator: Small frosted lemon slice icon in conversation header when privacy view active for that conversation
indicator.global_indicator: Lemon slice icon in app status bar area when global privacy view enabled

phase_1.name: Core Encrypted Messaging
deliverables: Server-side message deletion on delivery ack

phase_2.name: Ephemeral Messages + Screenshot Protection + UI Privacy Features
deliverables: Platform warning system — session platform tracking, real-time WebSocket update, warning badge in conversation header
Privacy view mode — frosted lemon-citrus blur, all three reveal modes, global + per-conversation toggle, notification suppression when active

fable_instructions.note: Instructions for Claude Code / Fable to build this project end-to-end
fable_instructions.start_here: Read this entire JSON before writing a single line of code
order: 1. Scaffold monorepo with pnpm workspaces and all root directories
2. Write all open source root files: LICENSE AGPL-3.0 full text, README.md, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, AUDIT.md, CHANGELOG.md
4. Write all docs: docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md, docs/SETUP.md, docs/SELF_HOSTING.md
5. Build packages/protocol — TypeScript types and message schemas
6. Build packages/crypto — libsodium.js wrappers for all key operations
7. Build packages/ui — LemonSlice SVG animated component FIRST, then all other components from design_system.components
8. Build server in Go — database schema sqlc, WebSocket hub, REST endpoints, JWT auth, platform session tracking for warning system

9. Build apps/web — React + Vite, integrate packages/crypto + packages/ui, full chat UI with screenshot protection, invisible watermark, platform warning badge, privacy view mode with frosted lemon-citrus blur
10. Build apps/android — Kotlin + Compose, FLAG_SECURE, libsignal-client, OkHttp pinning, platform warning badge, privacy view mode with lemon-citrus blur
11. Build apps/ios — SwiftUI, libsignal-client, Keychain, capture detection overlay, platform warning badge, privacy view mode with lemon-citrus blur
12. Build website — Next.js + Tailwind + Framer Motion marketing site for sublemonable.com per marketing_site spec
15. Final pass: verify every file in open_source.root_files_to_generate exists and is complete
critical_rules: NEVER log message content anywhere server or client
NEVER store plaintext keys always encrypted at rest
NEVER skip nonce generation every encrypted message needs a unique nonce
NEVER reuse keys one-time prekeys are single-use by design
NEVER add analytics crash reporting or telemetry of any kind
NEVER use white backgrounds in UI minimum dark value is #1A1800
NEVER use blue for interactive elements lemon yellow #F5E642 owns all interactivity
ALWAYS delete server-side message immediately after delivery ack
ALWAYS apply FLAG_SECURE on every Android Activity with message content
ALWAYS use parameterized SQL queries no string concatenation
ALWAYS validate JWT on every authenticated endpoint
ALWAYS rotate refresh tokens on use
ALWAYS apply AGPL-3.0 license header comment to every source file
Every loading state uses the LemonSlice spinner from packages/ui no other loaders
The burn animation uses particle dissolve upward not an opacity fade
Screenshot blur triggers in 120ms max it must feel jarring and protective
Key fingerprints always render in JetBrains Mono
Notification content is always New message never preview message content
Platform metadata travels as session signal ONLY — NEVER embedded in message envelopes — E2EE must not be affected
Platform warning badge is informative yellow — never red, never a modal, never blocking UI
Privacy view blur uses frosted lemon-citrus texture — not a generic grey blur
When privacy view is active notification previews are suppressed to sender name only — no exceptions
fable_instructions.design_reference: See design_system, marketing_site, and fable_ui_instructions keys in this file for full UI/UX spec. Build the LemonSlice SVG component in packages/ui FIRST before any screen work. It is used in logo, loader, burn timer, security badge, and send button.

open_source.license_rationale: Anyone running a modified Sublemonable as a service must open source their changes. Prevents silent commercial forks. Same license used by Signal.

LICENSE.note: Fetch full AGPL-3.0 text from https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.txt and write verbatim

sections: Hero — lemon slice logo + tagline: Nothing lasts. That's the point.
What is Sublemonable — 2 paragraph plain-language description
Security model — bullet summary linking to docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md
Features — E2EE, burn-on-read, screenshot protection, no phone number required

SECURITY.md.purpose: Responsible disclosure policy for security researchers

sections.title: Reporting a vulnerability
sections.content: Do NOT open a public issue. Use GitHub private security advisories.

sections.title: What to include
sections.content: Vulnerability description, reproduction steps, potential impact, suggested fix if any

sections.title: Response timeline
sections.content: Acknowledgement within 48 hours. Fix target within 90 days of confirmed vulnerability.

sections.title: Scope in scope
sections.content: server, web app, iOS app, Android app, packages/crypto, packages/protocol

sections.title: Recognition
sections.content: Valid disclosures credited in AUDIT.md and release notes unless researcher prefers anonymity

sections.title: Safe harbor
sections.content: Good-faith research under this policy is authorized. We will not pursue legal action against researchers who follow it.

sections.title: Before contributing
sections.content: Read docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md. All contributions must maintain zero-knowledge architecture.

sections.title: Hard rules never do this
items: Add server-side message logging of any kind
Add analytics, telemetry, or crash reporting
Change cryptography without a detailed explanation reviewed by a maintainer

sections.title: PR process
items: Open PR with clear description of what and why

AUDIT.md.initial_content: No audits conducted yet. This file updates as audits complete. Contact us via SECURITY.md if you want to conduct an audit.

docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md.purpose: Full technical security model for users and auditors
sections: Architecture overview with zero knowledge diagram
Key generation and storage per platform
What the server stores and provably cannot store
Transport security TLS 1.3 and certificate pinning
Threat model protected and out of scope

design_system.name: Sublemonable Design System
design_system.philosophy: Secure doesn't have to feel cold. Sublemonable is sharp, bright, and alive — like biting into a lemon. Every screen should feel like privacy is a feature you want, not a compromise you made.

display.usage: App name, hero headlines, section titles

signature_element.name: The Lemon Slice
signature_element.description: A segmented circle that appears as the logo mark, loading indicator, burn timer, and security badge. Drawn with SVG — segments fill with lemon-yellow as encryption handshake completes, as a message timer counts down, or as security level increases. It's the heartbeat of the app — every moment of waiting, protecting, or destroying is shown through this one motif.
variants: burn_timer — segments extinguish as TTL counts down, final segment triggers destruction animation
security_indicator — segments fill based on verification level (0-8)
send_button — holds the lemon slice, pulses on press

burn_on_read.indicator: Small flame icon on bubble corner, lemon-orange color
burn_on_read.on_open: Bubble catches fire animation — particles dissolve upward, text fades, bubble shrinks to nothing over 600ms

timed.indicator: Mini lemon-slice burn timer in corner of bubble
timed.warning_pulse: Lemon glow pulses when <10% time remains

screenshot_blur.web: filter: blur(24px) saturate(0) — applied instantly on visibilitychange/blur
screenshot_blur.description: Entire message list blurs — layout remains visible, content does not

burn_timer_ring.description: The lemon slice SVG used as countdown indicator on timed messages
burn_timer_ring.animation: Each segment fades out sequentially as TTL expires. Final segment triggers destruction animation.

preview_text.text: Always shows 'Encrypted message' — never previews content

passphrase_screen.center_element: Large lemon slice logo, segments illuminate as passphrase strength increases

input.style: minimal underline-only input, lemon-yellow on focus

passphrase_screen.strength_indicator: Lemon slice fills segment by segment

key_fingerprint.format: Groups of 4 hex chars separated by spaces

splash.center: Lemon slice logo — segments animate in clockwise, then pulse once
splash.wordmark: SUBLEMONABLE in Clash Display 700, lemon yellow, tracked wide

slides.visual: Animated lemon slice — segments = encryption layers
slides.headline: End-to-end encrypted
slides.body: Your messages are locked before they leave your device. We can't read them. Nobody can.

slides.headline: Messages that disappear
slides.body: Set any message to self-destruct. Once read, once seen, then gone.

slides.headline: Screenshots? Blocked.
slides.body: On Android, screenshots are impossible. On iOS and browser, we blur instantly.

slides.headline: No phone number needed
slides.body: Add contacts by QR code or link. We don't need your number, your email, or your name.

chat_list.header: SUBLEMONABLE wordmark left-aligned, settings icon right
chat_list.fab: Compose button — lemon yellow circle, bottom right, lemon-glow shadow

header.actions: verify key (lemon slice icon), burn all (flame icon), back

chat_view.encryption_notice: Persistent micro-badge at top: '🔒 End-to-end encrypted' in muted lemon

settings.style: Dark grouped list, lemon accents on active toggles
sections: Privacy — default TTL, burn-on-read default, screenshot watermark
Network — Tor routing toggle, connection status

animation_moments.message_send: Bubble scales from compose bar with spring easing, slides up
animation_moments.message_burn: Flame particles emit upward from bubble, bubble chars and shrinks to a lemon-slice ash icon, then fades
animation_moments.encryption_handshake: Lemon slice segments illuminate one by one on first message to new contact
animation_moments.screenshot_blur: Frosted glass effect slides over message list in 120ms — fast and jarring on purpose
animation_moments.key_verified: Lemon slice fully illuminates in green with a single ring pulse
animation_moments.app_unlock: Biometric success: lemon slice opens like a iris, reveals chat list

brand_voice.tone: Dry, sharp, confident. Never corporate. Never paranoid. Security is effortless here.

good: Encrypted before it leaves your hands.
Screenshots get blurred. Snoops get nothing.

sections.visual: Animated lemon slice — large, centered, slowly rotating, casting lemon-yellow glow on dark background
sections.headline: Nothing lasts.
sections.body: Sublemonable is end-to-end encrypted messaging that disappears. No logs. No screenshots. No trace.

items.headline: Encrypted before it leaves your hands.
items.body: Signal Protocol. Every message gets its own key. We store nothing readable. Not because the law says so — because it's technically impossible.
items.visual: Animated key exchange diagram — two lemon slices passing encrypted segments between them

items.headline: Read it. Watch it go.
items.body: Burn-on-read. Timed destruction. 30 seconds to a week — your call. The server deletes the moment your recipient gets it.

items.headline: Screenshots? We blur those.
items.body: Android blocks them entirely. iOS and browser blur your messages the instant a screenshot is attempted. We also watermark every conversation — if something leaks, we know who did it.
items.visual: Split mockup: left phone blurred, right shows 'Screenshot blocked'

items.headline: No number. No email. No name.
items.body: Connect by QR code. Your identity is a key pair we generate on your device. We don't know who you are.
items.visual: QR code scan animation with lemon-slice overlay

steps.label: Generate keys
steps.description: Your device creates your identity. Keys never leave your device.

steps.label: Exchange codes
steps.description: Share a QR or link. No personal info changes hands.

steps.label: Send encrypted
steps.description: Every message encrypted locally before sending.

steps.label: Delivered and deleted
steps.description: Server purges message the instant it's delivered.

steps.label: Gone forever
steps.description: Set to burn, or burn on read. Nothing stays.

sections.headline: We show our work.
sections.body: Sublemonable is open source. The encryption, the server, the apps — all of it auditable. Trust shouldn't require faith.

download.content: App store badges + PWA install prompt + self-host instructions

visual_motifs.lemon_slice_hero: Large SVG lemon slice, slow ambient rotation, yellow glow on dark background
visual_motifs.particle_field: Subtle floating lemon-yellow particles on dark bg — like citrus oils in light
visual_motifs.message_preview: Animated mockup phones showing messages burning
visual_motifs.code_reveal: TypeScript/Go encryption code snippet with syntax highlighting in lemon theme

fable_ui_instructions.design_tokens_file: packages/ui/src/tokens.ts — export all color, type, spacing tokens
fable_ui_instructions.tailwind_config: Extend Tailwind with all brand tokens as custom properties
fable_ui_instructions.dark_mode: Dark only — no light mode in v1
fable_ui_instructions.font_loading: Preload Clash Display + Inter + JetBrains Mono via <link rel=preload>
fable_ui_instructions.lemon_slice_svg: Build as reusable React component with animated segments prop (0-8 filled)
critical_rules: NEVER use white backgrounds — minimum is #1A1800
NEVER use blue for any interactive element — lemon yellow owns interactivity
Message content is ALWAYS dark bg (#242100) with light text or lemon bg with dark text — never inverted
The burn animation must feel satisfying — not a simple opacity fade
Screenshot blur must be jarring and instant — 120ms max — it should feel protective
Every loading state uses the lemon slice spinner
Key fingerprints always render in JetBrains Mono
Compose bar send button is always lemon yellow — it is the primary action color
Platform warning badge is lemon yellow informative tone — never red, never a modal, never blocks UI
Privacy view blur is frosted lemon-citrus texture (backdrop-filter + lemon tint rgba(245,230,66,0.08)) — never generic grey
Privacy view bubble shapes and layout remain visible — only text content obscured
Privacy view reveal is 150ms blur-out, 200ms blur-in — feel responsive not sluggish
Privacy view active indicator is small frosted lemon slice icon in conversation header
